items.title: Reflection inks $1B compute deal with Nebius
items.raw_content: Reflection AI has signed a $1 billion deal to access Nebius' compute. Reflection was founded in 2024 and is developing open source AI technology.
items.summary: Reflection AI has signed a $1 billion deal to access Nebius' compute. Reflection was founded in 2024 and is developing open source AI technology.

items.title: DeepMind CEO calls for an independent standards body to regulate frontier AI
items.raw_content: DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis is proposing an AI "standards body" modeled after FINRA, to test frontier models and develop best practices for their release.
items.summary: DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis is proposing an AI "standards body" modeled after FINRA, to test frontier models and develop best practices for their release.

items.title: Siri AI is already changing how I use my iPhone
items.raw_content: iOS 27 escaped the developer world today with the launch of the first public beta. I've been testing the new operating system since early June, looking for quirks and seeing if it can live up to the hype Apple promised in the keynote. This year's iOS upgrades are what one might call a Snow Leopard […]

items.title: Spotify expands its AI push with a ChatGPT-like music assistant
items.raw_content: Spotify is rolling out a new AI-powered conversational feature that lets Premium subscribers chat with the app to discover music, podcasts, audiobooks, and more.
items.summary: Spotify is rolling out a new AI-powered conversational feature that lets Premium subscribers chat with the app to discover music, podcasts, audiobooks, and more.

items.title: Sam Altman didn’t need another lawsuit
items.raw_content: OpenAI has spent the better part of the year involved in lawsuit after lawsuit, including one from the world's richest man. But last Friday, the company was hit with one of the highest-profile legal actions yet - from Apple. OpenAI's expensive hardware bet is what's on the line. Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI, filed in Northern […]

items.title: Google’s Demis Hassabis says it’s time for a global AI watchdog — led by the US
items.raw_content: Demis Hassabis thinks the world needs an AI watchdog with the power to hit the brakes if frontier models become too dangerous. Writing in a blog post, the Google DeepMind CEO and cofounder said the US should lead the initiative, arguing that the country is the best place to set global standards "given its economic […]

items.title: Google faces another AI training lawsuit from major publishers
items.raw_content: Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier, and other publishers allege that Google trained its AI on copyrighted works without the necessary permissions.
items.summary: Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier, and other publishers allege that Google trained its AI on copyrighted works without the necessary permissions.

items.title: Meta’s Adam Mosseri says AI token budgets could soon be capped per engineer
items.raw_content: Instagram head Adam Mosseri believes companies will eventually need to manage AI token spending the same way they manage payroll or other operating expenses, predicting that engineers could soon face limits on how much they spend using AI tools.
items.summary: Instagram head Adam Mosseri believes companies will eventually need to manage AI token spending the same way they manage payroll or other operating expenses, predicting that engineers could soon face

items.title: Google Images gets a Pinterest-like redesign focused on discovery
items.raw_content: Now, when users navigate to Google Images, they'll see a "For You" gallery of images tailored to their interests and browsing history.
items.summary: Now, when users navigate to Google Images, they'll see a "For You" gallery of images tailored to their interests and browsing history.

items.title: New York State halts construction of all new data centers
items.raw_content: New York has become the first state to temporarily halt approval of large data centers, as Gov. Kathy Hochul argues the AI-driven building boom shouldn’t come at the expense of higher electricity costs, water supplies, or local control.
items.summary: New York has become the first state to temporarily halt approval of large data centers, as Gov. Kathy Hochul argues the AI-driven building boom shouldn’t come at the expense of higher electricity cost

items.title: The real AI race may no longer be at the frontier
items.raw_content: Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue says enterprises increasingly want open models, due to cost, accessibility, and ownership. Do frontier models still matter if most production AI ends up running on open models?

items.title: Superhuman’s new auto-draft feature almost makes me like AI replies
items.raw_content: Superhuman’s latest AI email drafting feature is its most convincing yet, generating replies that often required little to no editing in our testing.
items.summary: Superhuman’s latest AI email drafting feature is its most convincing yet, generating replies that often required little to no editing in our testing.

items.title: Already rich, already successful, why the last wave of tech winners is grinding again
items.raw_content: They're rolling up their sleeves again, seemingly out of fear of missing AI's defining moment and, presumably, the irresistible allure of making even more money -- potentially a lot more.
items.summary: They're rolling up their sleeves again, seemingly out of fear of missing AI's defining moment and, presumably, the irresistible allure of making even more money -- potentially a lot more.

items.title: Meta accused of using biased AI targeting for mass layoffs
items.raw_content: A group of 26 former Meta employees is suing the company over claims that it used AI tools to unfairly target workers on leave with layoffs, as reported earlier by Reuters. In the lawsuit, the employees allege Meta determined which workers to dismiss based on performance data collected by a "constellation" of internal AI tools, […]

items.title: Spotify is now an AI chatbot, too
items.raw_content: Spotify is experimenting with a new AI feature that allows Premium subscribers to play and explore music, audiobooks, and podcasts by having conversations with a chatbot. items.title: New York becomes the first state to enact a data center moratorium
items.raw_content: New hyperscale data centers can't set up shop in New York for up to a year now that Governor Kathy Hochul (D) has signed the nation's first statewide moratorium. But a bill passed by the state legislature that could restrict even more developments still awaits her signature. The order blocks new environmental permits for data […]

items.title: The Google Images homepage will recommend photos even before you search
items.raw_content: Google is announcing a big change to the Google Images homepage in honor of the platform's 25th anniversary this week. items.raw_content: When Apple employees interviewed for jobs at OpenAI, the AI startup's hardware head allegedly asked them to show up with something unusual: components they were working on and unreleased product samples.
